title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 17 17

questions.question: Amar has sixteen coins of two-rupee denomination and nine coins of five-rupee denomination. While coming from the market he lost three coins of each denomination. What is the total amount left with Amar now?
questions.explanation: The amount with Amar initially = 16 x 2 + 9 x 5 = 32 + 45 = 77. The amount lost = 3 x 2 + 3 x 5 = 6 + 15 = 21. The amount left = 77 - 21 = 56.

questions.question: Queue A at a cinema has 27 people. Queue B is behind it. In Queue B, Rohan is 9th from the back. After Queue A is placed completely in front of Queue B ,no change in internal orders. Rohan becomes 41st from the front in the combined queue. How many people are in Queue B?
questions.explanation: Let b = number of people in Queue B Rohan's position from the front within Queue B = 41 − 27 = 14 In Queue B, total = (front rank) + (back rank) − 1 b = 14 + 9 − 1 = 22

questions.question: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. i. Kingfisher ii. Kidding iii. Kidney iv. Kick
questions.explanation: The correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary is Kick, Kidding, Kidney, Kingfisher.

questions.question: Choose the address that is exactly the same as the one given below. Room No. 204, Block B, Sunrise Hostel, IIT Madras, Chennai, Tamil Nadu - 600036
options: Room No. 204, Block B, Sunrise Hostel, IIT Madras, Chennai, Tamil Nadu - 600036
Room No. 204, Block B, Sunrise Hostel, IIT Madras, Chennai, Tamilnadu - 600036
Room No. 204, Block B, Sunrise Hostel, IIT Madras, Chennai, Tamil Nadu - 600063
Room No. 240, Block B, Sunrise Hostel, IIT Madras, Chennai, Tamil Nadu - 600036
questions.explanation: Option a, matches exactly in room number, block, hostel name, institution name, city, state formatting, and PIN. Option b, merges the state name (Tamilnadu instead of Tamil Nadu). Option c, changes the PIN code (600063 instead of 600036). Option d, changes the room number (240 instead of 204).

questions.question: Which pair of numbers has a sum of 29 and whose squares differ by 203?
questions.explanation: By options: a) 20 and 9 difference = 400 - 81 = 319 b) 18 and 11 difference = 324 - 121 = 203 c) 22 and 7 difference = 484 - 49 = 435 d) 16 and 13 difference = 256 - 169 = 87 Hence option b is correct.

questions.question: Read the given information and decide the appropriate course of action. A company is hiring a security officer and has laid the following conditions for a candidate to be eligible for the interview. I. The candidate must have attained the minimum age of 33 years and must not have attained 45 years on the first day of September of the year 2019. II. The candidate should be an officer with a minimum of five years of commissioned service in the Army, Navy, or Air force. III. The candidate should have passed the entrance exam with a minimum of 65%. Candidate 1: Daljeet, who was born on November 6, 1983 and was commissioned in the Navy in the year 2012. Daljeet continued in the Navy till 2018 and got 215 marks out of 350 in the entrance exam. Candidate 2: Gary, who celebrated his 42nd birthday in 2017 has experience of seven years in the Air force. Gary gave the entrance test with Daljeet and got twenty-seven marks more than Daljeet.
options: Neither candidate 1 nor candidate 2 are eligible
questions.explanation: Candidate 1: The candidate 1 is not eligible as Daljeet fulfills the first two conditions but could not fulfill the third one. In the entrance test he got 61% which is less than 65%. Candidate 2: The candidate 2 is eligible as he fulfills all three conditions. Gary got 242 marks out of 350 which is 69%. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices be represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'D' can be represented by 02, 14 etc. and 'T' can be represented by 57, 69 etc. Similarly, you have identify the set for the word 'FOREST'. MATRIX I 01234 0PODSE 1OESPD 2DPEOS 3SDPEO 4ESODP 56789 5RZTFL 6LFZRT 7ZTRLF 8FRLTZ 9TLFZR
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have F = 58, 66, 79, 85, 97 O = 01, 10, 23, 34, 42 R = 55, 68, 77, 86, 99 E = 04, 11, 22, 33, 40 S =03, 12, 24, 30, 41 T = 57, 69, 76, 88, 95 Only the option 85, 42, 77, 22, 03, 69 has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: In a certain code language, X ♪ Y means 'X is the mother-in-law of Y' X + Y means 'X is the father of Y' X ≈ Y means 'X is the only child of Y' X ÷ Y means 'X is the husband of Y' Based on the above, how is J related to L if 'L ≈ N ÷ T ♪ G + W ÷ J'?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25528/image001.png) L ≈ N ÷ T ♪ G + W ÷ J L ≈ N means 'L is the only child of N' N ÷ T means 'N is the husband of T' T ♪ G means 'T is the mother-in-law of G' G + W means 'G is the father of W' W ÷ J means 'W is the husband of J' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25528/image011.png) J is the daughter-in-law of L.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 4, 10, 30, 38, 190, 200, 1400, ?
Which of the following letter clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? CX, VE, GT, RI,?
questions.explanation: ABCDEFGHIJKLM ZYXWVUTSRQPON So, 'KP' is the correct option to complete the following letter series.

questions.question: Pointing to a boy playing on the ground Pratik said, "He is my brother's daughter's mother's husband's son". How is that boy related to Pratik?
questions.explanation: Pointing to a boy playing on the ground Pratik said, "He is my brother's daughter's mother's husband's son". ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25407/image004.png) That boy is the brother's son of Pratik.

questions.question: Select the odd group of numbers. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is NOT allowed.)
Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of the last figure. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20490/image003.jpg)
questions.explanation: Figure II is the correct unfolded figure. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20490/image004.jpg)

questions.question: Which of the following letter clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? CEJBDR, DGMFIX, EIPJND, FKSNSJ, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: C+1=D; D+1=E; E+1=F. So, F+1=G. E+2=G; G+2=I; I+2=K. So, K+2=M. J+3=M; M+3=P; P+3=S. So, S+3=V. B+4=F; F+4=J; J+4=N. So, N+4=R. D+5=I; I+5=N; N+5=S. So, S+5=X. R+6=X; X+6=D; D+6=J. So, J+6=P.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 1, 2, 6, 42, ?
Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially replace the * signs and balance the given equation. 210 * 42 * 21 * 84 * 7 * 28
questions.explanation: Option (a): ÷, X , =, - , + 210 ÷ 42 X 21 = 84 - 7 + 28 105 = 105 LHS is equal to RHS. Thus, option (a) is correct. Option (b): - , X , +, ÷, = 210 - 42 X 21 + 84 ÷ 7 * 28 210 - 882 + 12 = 28 -660 ≠ 28 LHS is not equal to RHS. Thus, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): =, +, ÷, X , - 210 = 42 + 21 ÷ 84 X 7 - 28 210 = 42 + 1.75 - 28 210 ≠ 15.75 LHS is not equal to RHS. Thus, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d): ÷, +, - , X , = 210 ÷ 42 + 21 - 84 X 7 = 28 5 + 21 - 588 = 28 -562 ≠ 28 LHS is not equal to RHS. Thus, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: If A stands for '+', B stands for '÷', C stands for ' - ', D' stands for 'x', then the value of which of the following expression is 2?
questions.explanation: A stands for '+', B stands for '÷', C stands for ' - ', D' stands for 'x', then the expressions will be: Option (a) 7 - 3 x 6 ÷ 2 + 9 = 7 - 9 + 9 = 16 - 9 = 7 So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b) 15 ÷ 5 x 6 + 11 - 3 = 18 + 11 - 3 = 29 - 3 = 26 So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c) 12 x 6 - 18 x 2 + 4 = 72 - 36 + 4 = 76 - 36 = 40 So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d) 14 + 18 ÷ 2 - 7 x 3 = 14 + 9 - 21 = 23 - 21 = 2 So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: If in a word BANKRUPTCY, every alternate letter starting from B are dropped. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is fourth from the left and the one which is first from the right in the new letter-cluster thus formed?
questions.explanation: The given word is BANKRUPTCY. Every alternate letter starting from B are dropped. So, we get: AKUTY The fourth letter from left is T and the first letter from right is Y. So, letters between T and Y as in the English alphabetical series is U, V, W and X. So, there are 4 such letters.

questions.question: If C + D means C is the father of D, C * D means C is the wife of D, C & D means C is the sister of D, C ^ D means C is the husband of B, which of the following shows that A is the mother-in-law of B?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26576/image003.png) Option (a): A ^ H & M * B A ^ H means A is the husband of H H & M means H is the sister of M M * B means M is the wife of B ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26576/image010.png) A is the brother-in-law of B. So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b): A + N * Q + B A + N means A is the father of N N * Q means N is the wife of Q Q + B means Q is the father of B ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26576/image011.png) A is the grandfather of B. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): A * S + N ^ B A * S means A is the wife of S S + N means S is the father of N N ^ B means N is the husband of B ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26576/image012.png) A is the mother-in-law of. So, option (c) is correct. Option (d): A ^ K & S * B A ^ K means A is the husband of K K & S means K is the sister of S S * B means S is the wife of B ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26576/image013.png) A is the brother-in-law of B. So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: In the following question, eight words are given and seven of them are alike in some manner. Select the option which shows the odd word. Rome, Wellington, New Delhi, Moscow, Tokyo, Melbourne, Hanoi, Dhaka
questions.explanation: All cities except Melbourne are capital cities of their respective countries and Melbourne is just a city in Australia.

questions.question: Which of the following words can be formed by using the letters of the word "BLACKBOARD"?
questions.explanation: Only DARK can be formed from the given options by using the letters of the word "BLACKBOARD".

questions.question: In the question below, three statements are given followed by conclusions: I, II and III. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read the conclusions and decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some lost are woods. Some woods are forests. All woods are green. Conclusions: I. No green is lost. II. Some forests are green. III. Some lost are green.
options: II and either I or III follows
questions.explanation: Some lost are woods. Some woods are forests. All woods are green. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8227/image012.jpg) No green is lost is not true. So, I does not follow. Some forests are green is true. So, II follows. Some lost are green is true. So, III follows. Hence, both II and III follow.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 42: 162 :: 64: 184 :: 28: ?
questions.explanation: 42: 42+120= 162 64: 64 +120 = 184 Similarly, 28: 28 + 120 = 148 So, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: If DROP is coded as HIND BLUR ECHO YAWP, then what code can be considered as BUSY?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: DROP is coded as HIND BLUR ECHO YAWP. D = HIND; Here, the word ends with D. R = BLUR; Here, the word ends with R. O = ECHO; Here, the word ends with O. P = YAWP; Here, the word ends with P. Also, none of the letters in the code is repeated. Following the same pattern, option (c) is the code for BUSY. B = JAMB; Here, the word ends with B. U = ECRU; Here, the word ends with U. S = ANTS; Here, the word ends with S. Y = FOGY; Here, the word ends with Y. Also, none of the letters in the code is repeated.

questions.question: In a certain code language, if PAGE is coded as 11262022, then what will be the code of CODE in that language?
questions.explanation: We know that, ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 ZYXWVUTSRQPON 26252423222120191817161514 PAGE is coded as 11262022. Here, the pattern is: The reverse alphabet of P according to the English alphabetical series is K and K = 11. The reverse alphabet of A according to the English alphabetical series is Z and Z = 26. The reverse alphabet of G according to the English alphabetical series is T and T = 20. The reverse alphabet of E according to the English alphabetical series is V and V = 22. Similarly, The reverse alphabet of C according to the English alphabetical series is X and X = 24. The reverse alphabet of O according to the English alphabetical series is L and L = 12. The reverse alphabet of D according to the English alphabetical series is W and W = 23. The reverse alphabet of E according to the English alphabetical series is V and V = 22.

questions.question: When Ethyl alcohol is reacted with oxygen it forms ____.
questions.explanation: · Reaction: CH₃CH₂OH₍ₗ₎ + O₂₍g₎ à CH₃COOH ₍ₐq₎ + H₂O₍ₗ₎ · Ethyl alcohol and oxygen produce acetic acid and water. · Significance: Wine becomes sour with age and exposure to the air. This is due to the conversion of alcohol to acetic acid (vinegar).

questions.question: Read the statements regarding the Fundamental Rights and select the correct option: Statement 1 - There are seven fundamental rights. Statement 2 - They are contained in part IV of the constitution of India.
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights - - There are six fundamental rights in the constitution of India. - Fundamental rights are justiciable and are protected by the judiciary. - They are contained in part III if the constitution. Therefore, Neither statement 1 nor statement 2 is true.

questions.question: The sex ratio at birth in India was roughly ______ boys per 100 girls in the 2011 census.
questions.explanation: The sex ratio at birth in India, which was roughly 111 boys per 100 girls in the 2011 census, has since narrowed to about 109 in the 2015 - 16 National Family Health Survey wave and to 108 boys in the most recent wave of the NFHS, which was performed from 2019 - 21.

questions.question: Which state secured the top rank among the 'Coastal/Large States' category in the Export Preparedness Index (EPI) 2024 released by NITI Aayog in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra topped the Export Preparedness Index (EPI) 2024 among large/coastal states, as per the NITI Aayog report released in January 2026. The EPI 2024 is a ranking prepared by NITI Aayog to assess how well states are prepared and equipped to export goods. Tamil Nadu - It ranked 2nd among large/coastal states in EPI 2024 Gujarat - Ranked 3rd among large states in EPI 2024, Further Insights Maharashtra's lead in EPI 2024 reflects its diversified export base and strong connectivity from major ports like Mumbai and JNPT. EPI 2024 uses 70 indicators across pillars like business ecosystem, export infrastructure, policy & governance and export performance. Tamil Nadu and Gujarat continue to be major export contributors for India, but ranked 2nd and 3rd respectively in 2024. Uttar Pradesh is notable as a non-coastal state showing strong export potential in the large states category. Uttarakhand topped the small states/UT category in EPI 2024.

questions.question: A community that is in near equilibrium with the environment is called a:
questions.explanation: An important characteristic of all communities is that their composition and structure constantly change in response to the changing environmental conditions. This change is orderly and sequential, parallel with the changes in the physical environment. These changes lead finally to a community that is in near equilibrium with the environment and that is called a climax community. The gradual and fairly predictable change in the species composition of a given area is called ecological succession.

questions.question: Which of the following orbital parameters of the Earth is NOT part of the Milankovitch Cycles that influence long-term climate change?
questions.explanation: Eccentricity refers to how elliptical Earth's orbit is. It varies on a ~100,000-year cycle. Influences how much solar radiation the Earth receives at different times of year. Obliquity is the tilt of Earth's axis, which changes between 22.1° and 24.5°. Occurs over ~41,000 years. Strongly affects seasonal intensity. Precession is the wobble of Earth's axis. Occurs on a ~23,000-year cycle. Changes the timing of seasons relative to Earth's orbit. Orbital inclination refers to the tilt of Earth's orbital plane relative to the Sun's equator. Although it does change over long time scales (~100,000-year cycles), it is not included in the traditional three Milankovitch parameters. It has far weaker climatic influence compared to eccentricity, obliquity, and precession. Further Insights · Milankovitch cycles are key drivers of ice age cycles on Earth. · Eccentricity affects the distance from the Sun, influencing total solar energy received. · Obliquity mainly controls seasonal contrast between summers and winters. · Precession determines when seasons occur during Earth's orbit.

questions.question: The Rourkela Steel plant was set up in 1959 in the Sundargarh district of Odisha in collaboration with
questions.explanation: The Rourkela Steel plant was set up in 1959 in the Sundargarh district of Odisha in collaboration with Germany. The plant was located on the basis of proximity to raw materials, thus, minimising the cost of transporting weight losing raw material. This plant has a unique locational advantage, as it receives coal from Jharia (Jharkhand) and iron ore from Sundargarh and Kendujhar. The Hirakud project supplies power for the electric furnaces and water is obtained from the Koel and Sankh rivers.

questions.question: Shruti Sadolikar Katkar is a renowned Khayal singer from _______Gharana.
questions.explanation: Shruti Sadolikar Katkar is a Khayal singer from Jaipur-Atrauli Gharana, who bagged the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award for Hindustani vocal music in 2011.

questions.question: Why is PC Mahalanobis' name talked about in the context of Five-Year Plans?
options: He was the chairman of the Planning Commission
He established the Institute of Economic Growth
He conceptualised the framework for the Second Five-Year Plan
He was the finance minister during the First Five-Year Plan period
questions.explanation: P C Mahalanobis is justly considered integral to the story of planning in India. He was the author of the crucial Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61), whose underlying strategy-of a vast public sector and import substitution industrialization-later passed into policy orthodoxy. It defined the Indian economy right until the market reforms of the early 1990s.

questions.question: Where has India's first open-sea marine fisheries project been launched?
questions.explanation: India's first open-sea marine fish farming project was launched in the Andaman Sea, specifically at North Bay, Sri Vijaya Puram off the coast of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. This project marks the first time India has done fish farming out in the open ocean (not close to the shore), using special cages for marine fish and seaweed cultivation. Further Insights The project is part of India's Blue Economy initiative to boost ocean based livelihoods and economic growth. It uses open-sea cage technology developed by the National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT).

questions.question: The difference between the government's total expenditure and its total receipts excluding borrowing is called_______.
questions.explanation: The difference between the government's total expenditure and its total receipts excluding borrowing is called fiscal deficit. Gross fiscal deficit = Total expenditure - (Revenue receipts + Non-debt creating capital receipts) Generally, fiscal deficit takes place either due to revenue deficit or a major hike in capital expenditure.

questions.question: Solung festival is celebrated by which of the following tribes in Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Solung is the most popular festival of the Adis of Arunachal Pradesh which is celebrated on September 1 every year. Though primarily an agro-based festival, it also reflects the socio-religious features of the people. Losar, Dree, Si-Donyi, Mopin, Nyokum, Reh, Boori Boot, Chalo Loku, Sangken are some other festivals of the state.

questions.question: The term "Lai Haraoba", literally meaning "the merrymaking of gods", is associated with which of the following?
options: A tribal dance festival of Odisha
A Buddhist religious ceremony of Ladakh
questions.explanation: Lai Haraoba: · Literally means "merrymaking of gods" · Celebrated in Manipur · Performed to please traditional Meitei deities (Lai) · Involves ritual dances, music, and symbolic reenactment of creation myths Assam's major festivals include Bihu (Rongali, Kongali, Bhogali) Odisha is known for festivals like Chhau, Rath Yatra Ladakh's Buddhist ceremonies include Hemis, Losar

questions.question: If we have to pay Rs.80 for $1 then the forex rate is ________
questions.explanation: Foreign Exchange Rate (also called Forex Rate) is the price of one currency in terms of another. It links the currencies of different countries and enables comparison of international costs and prices. For example, if we have to pay Rs 80 for $1 then the exchange rate is Rs 80 per dollar.

questions.question: Match the following. Group A (Rivers)Group B (State of Origin) I. Godavaria. Uttarakhand II. Tapi b. Madhya Pradesh III. Yamunac. Karnataka IV. Cauveryd. Maharashtra
questions.explanation: Correct code is 1-d, 2-b, 3-c, 4-a. GodavariOriginates from Trimbakeshwar near Nasik in Maharashtra. TapiOriginates in the Eastern Satpura Range of southern Madhya Pradesh YamunaOriginates from the Yamunotri glacier at the Bandarpoonch peak in Uttarakhand CauveryOriginates in the Brahmagiri hills in Kodagu, Karnataka

questions.question: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT for Friction?
options: Ball bearings are used to reduce friction
Friction acts on the ball while moving in the air
The friction force does not act on fluid
questions.explanation: The frictional force on an object in a fluid depends on its speed with respect to the fluid. The frictional force also depends on the shape of the object and the nature of the fluid. Friction can produce heat. Ball bearings are used to reduce friction. Friction acts on the ball while moving in the air. Air friction is experienced by the objects moving through the open air. Air friction acts between the object and the air through which it is moving. It is also called drag.

questions.question: Chandragupta Maurya established Mauryan Empire (322 B.C.) by defeating ____.
questions.explanation: · Chandragupta Maurya was the founder of the Mauryan Empire. · He, at the young age of 25, captured Pataliputra from the last ruler of the Nanda dynasty, Dhanananda. · In this task he was assisted by Kautilya, who was also known as Chanakya or Vishnugupta.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Most of the west flowing rivers don't form deltas. Reason (R): High gradient of west flowing rivers. Select the appropriate code from below:
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation for A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation for A
A is true and R is false
A is false and R is true
questions.explanation: For deltas to form, high sedimentation is essential. As west flowing rivers flow in higher gradient and for shorter distance, their erosion and carrying capacity are low. And due to higher gradient they form estuaries.

questions.question: Identify the INCORRET statement(s) about the "Mansabdar". A. The term mansabdar refers to an individual who holds a mansab, meaning a position or rank. B. Rank and salary were determined by a numerical value called iqtas.
questions.explanation: The term mansabdar refers to an individual who holds a mansab, meaning a position or rank. It was a grading system used by the Mughals to fix (1) rank, (2) salary and (3) military responsibilities. · Rank and salary were determined by a numerical value called zat. · The higher the zat, the more prestigious was the noble's position in court and the larger his salary. · The mansabdar's military responsibilities required him to maintain a specified number of sawar or cavalrymen. · The mansabdar brought his cavalrymen for review, got them registered, their horses branded and then received money to pay them as salary. · Mansabdars received their salaries as revenue assignments called jagirs which were somewhat like iqtas.

questions.question: In which year were the first modern Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The inaugural modern Olympic Games took place in Athens, Greece, in 1896. These Games were organized and revived by Pierre de Coubertin, a French educator and founder of the International Olympic Committee (IOC). The modern Olympics were inspired by the ancient Olympic Games held in ancient Greece from the 8th century BCE to the 4th century CE.

questions.question: Which organ is the largest in the lymphatic system and is approximately the size of a clenched fist?
questions.explanation: The spleen is the biggest lymph organ and about the size of our fist. The spleen is the largest lymphatic organ in the human body. It plays a key role in: Filtering blood Recycling old red blood cells Supporting immune responses Anatomically, it is roughly the size of a clenched fist and lies in the upper left abdomen. Tonsils are part of the lymphatic system but are very small. Lymph nodes filter lymph, but the spleen filters blood. the thymus is smaller and most active only during childhood,It shrinks after puberty. Further Insights The spleen acts as a blood reservoir. It removes pathogens and damaged blood cells. Humans can survive without a spleen, but immunity becomes weaker. Lymphatic system is crucial for immunity and fluid balance. Enlargement of spleen is called splenomegaly.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India deals with ordinance making power of President of India?
questions.explanation: · Article 123: Power of President to promulgate Ordinances during recess of Parliament. · President is given powers to promulgate Ordinances when both the Houses or either of the Houses are not in session. · The Parliament has to approve the ordinance within six weeks from its reassembly else it lapses. · The acts done and completed under the ordinance before it lapses, remains fully active. Further insights: · Article 213: deals with ordinance making power of Governor of a state. · Article 341 deals with the Scheduled Castes (SCs) in India. It empowers the President of India to specify the castes, races, or tribes as Scheduled Castes for each state or union territory. · Article 143 grants the President of India the power to seek the opinion of the Supreme Court of India on certain legal or constitutional matters.

questions.question: Who became the second Indian after Viswanathan Anand to win the prestigious Tata Steel Masters Chess Tournament in 2025?
questions.explanation: Indian Grandmaster R. Praggnanandhaa won the Masters title at the 2025 Tata Steel Chess Tournament (held in Wijk aan Zee, Netherlands). In doing so, he became only the second Indian - after chess legend Viswanathan Anand - to win this prestigious event. Although D. Gukesh performed very well and even competed in tiebreaks, he lost to Praggnanandhaa and did not win the title. Further Insights The 2025 edition was the 87th Tata Steel Chess Tournament, held from January 17 to February 2, 2025. The Masters section is one of the most competitive chess tournaments in the world. Praggnanandhaa defeated fellow Indian Gukesh in a blitz tiebreak to clinch the title. Viswanathan Anand had previously been the only Indian to win the tournament, doing so multiple times in the 1990s and 2000s. Winning Tata Steel Masters elevated Praggnanandhaa's global ranking and cemented his reputation as one of the worlds top young grandmasters.

questions.question: Lacrosse is the national summer sport of
questions.explanation: Ice hockey is recognized and declared to be the national winter sport of Canada and the game commonly known as lacrosse is recognized and declared to be the national summer sport of Canada. Lacrosse is typically played on a field, with each team consisting of 10 players in field lacrosse (3 attack, 3 midfielders, 3 defensemen, and a goalie) or 6 players in box lacrosse (1 goalie and 5 outfield players). National sports of some countries: Canada - Lacrosse (summer) and Ice Hockey (winter) United States - American Football India - Field Hockey Brazil - Volleyball New Zealand - Rugby South Korea - Taekwondo Mexico - Charrera Japan - Sumo Wrestling England - Cricket Australia - Australian Rules Football (AFL) Pakistan - Field Hockey Thailand - Muay Thai Argentina - Pato (a game played on horseback, similar to polo) Sri Lanka - Volleyball Philippines - Arnis (Martial Arts) Nepal - Volleyball Bangladesh - Kabaddi South Africa - Rugby

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the 'Silver Notice': i) It is a newly introduced color-coded notice by INTERPOL, specifically designed to help member countries locate and seize criminal assets. ii) The first Silver Notice was issued following a request from India as part of a pilot project involving 52 countries. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement i is correct The Silver Notice is a newly introduced colour-coded notice published by INTERPOL in January 2025 to help trace, locate and recover criminal assets and proceeds of crime across borders. It enables member countries to share alerts/requests for information on assets linked to criminal activity so they can identify, trace and - under their national laws - seize or restrain those assets. Statement ii is incorrect The first Silver Notice published by Interpol (10 Jan 2025) was issued at the request of Italy, not India. India is part of the pilot group of countries using the mechanism, and India later used a Silver Notice in a separate case, but it did not request the inaugural notice. Further insights The Silver Notice pilot (involving ~52 countries) was launched so member states can collectively test the new tool before full rollout. Interpols Silver Notice can be used to request information about the location, identity or status of assets (movable or immovable) tied to criminal activity. The first Silver Notice published in January 2025 concerned alleged assets belonging to a senior organised-crime figure (the initial request came from Italy). Participation in the pilot allows member countries to collectively request multiple Silver Notices (Interpol indicated caps for pilot usage). Use of a Silver Notice does not itself seize assets - it facilitates information-sharing; any seizure or restraint must be carried out under national laws and procedures.

questions.question: Pipes A, B, and C alone can fill a tank in 51 minutes, 68 minutes, and 136 minutes, respectively while pipe D alone can empty the tank in 170 minutes. If pipe A and pipe D opened together and after 180/7 minutes pipe A is closed and pipes B and C are opened, then remaining part of the tank filled in how many minutes?
questions.explanation: Pipes A, B, and C alone can fill a tank in 51 minutes, 68 minutes, and 136 minutes, respectively while pipe D alone can empty the tank in 170 minutes Let remaining part of the tank will be filled in T minutes. 180/7 * (1/51 - 1/170) + T(1/68 + 1/136 - 1/170) = 1 => 6/17 + T * 11/680 = 1 => T = 680/11 * 11/17 => T = 40 minutes

questions.question: If the slope of a line passing through points P(2, -5) and Q(x, 4) respectively is (-⅔), then what is the value of (2x + 7)?
questions.explanation: According to the question: (-⅔) = (4 - (-5))/(x - 2) x = -23/2 Therefore, (2x + 7) = 2 * (-23/2) + 7 = -16

questions.question: The length of the external common tangent is 80 cm. The radii of the two circles are 62 cm and 23 cm. What is the distance between the two circles?
questions.explanation: The distance between two circles = D The length of the external common tangent = L L = √[(D² - (r¹ - r²)²] 80 = √[D² - (62 - 23)²] 80² = D² - 39² D² = 6400 + 1521 D² = 7921 D = 89 cm

questions.question: Simplify the following: [{6 ÷ 3_________4 of ( 2_________3 + 2)} ÷ 2 1_________4 + 5_________8 - 1_________2 ] ÷ 1 1_________3 + 8 ÷ 12 x 3
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image006.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image008.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image009.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image010.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17488/image011.png) = 35/32 + 2 = 99/32

questions.question: Ritu and Janaki started a restaurant by investing Rs. 97200 and Rs. 145800 respectively. After 9 months Shreya also joined them with an investment of Rs. 113400. What is the share of profit of Shreya, if at the end of the year, total profit is Rs. 268000?

A right circular cone of height 28 cm is cut by a parallel plane to its base such that the height of both the parts is equal. The volume of frustum of cone is 2508 cm³. If the radius of upper portion of the frustum of the cone is 6 cm, then find the radius of its lower portion. (take π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Height of cone = 28 cm Then, h = height of frustum of cone = 28/2 = 14 cm r = 6 cm Now, volume of frustum of cone = (⅓) * πh * (R² + rR + r²) 2508 = (⅓) * (22/7) * 14 * (R² + 6R + 6²) R = 9 cm

questions.question: Mohan borrowed a certain sum on simple interest and the same sum on compound interest at a certain rate of interest per annum. The ratio between the difference of compound interest and simple interest of 2 years and that of 3 years is 10: 33. The rate of interest is:
questions.explanation: Let the sum is Rs. P and rate of interest is r%. The difference between SI and CI for 2 years is P*(r/100)². The difference between SI and CI for 3 years is P* (r/100)²* (300+r)/100. According to question, [P*(r/100)²]/ [P* (r/100)²* (300+r)/100] = 10/33 100/ (300 + r) = 10/33 300 + r = 330 r = 30%

questions.question: A cylindrical vessel of radius 32 cm and height 35cm is full of water. Its contents are emptied into a rectangular tub of length 50cm and breadth 64 cm. The height (in cm) to which the water rises in the tub is:(take π = 22/7)

questions.question: In the figure given below, distance between centres of the circle is 30 cm, radius of circles is 7.5 cm and 5 cm respectively. Find the length of POR. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3466/image002.jpg)
questions.explanation: In △POA and △ROB, ∠PAO = ∠RBO = 90° ∠POA = ∠ROB = (Opposite Angles) So, △POA and △ROB are similar triangles. PA/RB = OA/OB = 7.5/5 = 3/2 OA = 30 * (⅗) = 18 cm and OB = 30 * (⅖) = 12 cm In △POA, OP² = PA² + OA² OP = √(56.25 + 324) = 19.5 cm In △ROB, OR² = RB² + OB² OR = √(25 + 144) = 13 cm POR = OP + OR = 19.5 + 13 = 32.5 cm

questions.question: Find the smallest number of five digits exactly divisible by 15, 30 and 36.
questions.explanation: Smallest number of five digits = 10000 Required number must be divisible by LCM of 15, 30 and 36 which is 180 Dividing 10000 by 180 we get 100 as remainder Required number = 10000 + (180 - 100) = 10080

questions.question: Pritam buys rice at the rate of Rs. 15/kg. He sells it to Rhitam at 20% profit. However his faulty balance shows 1 kg when it is actually 850 gm. Rhitam bought 20 Kgs of rice from Pritam. What is the overall profit (in Rs.) that Pritam makes?
questions.explanation: Pritam sells rice to Rhitam at a price of = (15+20% of 15)= Rs.18/kg. Now Rhitam bought 20 Kgs from Pritam at a total price of =Rs. 18*20 =Rs. 360 But as Pritam has a faulty balance thus he is actually selling 850*20/100=17Kgs instead of 20 Kgs. Pritam buys 17kgs of rice at a total price of =Rs.17*15 =Rs. 255 and sells it to Rhitam at a price of Rs.360 So Total profit that Pritam makes =Rs. (360 - 255) =Rs. 105.

questions.question: Find the value of sec A if cot A = cosec A_________4
questions.explanation: cotA = cosecA/4 cosA/sinA = 1/4sinA cosA = ¼ secA = 4

questions.question: If x + 1_________x = 3, then what is the value of x⁴ + 1_________x⁴ - x² - 1_________x²

The quantity of water and milk is in the ratio of 5:7. If 24 liters of water is added to the mixture, the ratio becomes 7:5. Which of the following statement/s is/are true? A: The initial quantity of milk in the mixture is 21 L. B: The Initial quantity of water in the mixture is 25 L
questions.explanation: Let the quantity of water and milk be 5m and 7m respectively. According to the question: (5m+24)/7m = 7/5 25m + 120 = 49m 24m = 120 m = 5 So, Initial quantity of water = 5* 5 = 25 litres And initial quantity of milk = 7 * 5 = 35 Litres Hence only statement B is ture.

questions.question: If 3ᵖ⁺³ * 3²ᵖ⁻¹ = 9ᵖ⁺⁶, then find the value of p².
questions.explanation: 3ᵖ⁺³ * 3²ᵖ⁻¹ = 9ᵖ⁺⁶ 3ᵖ⁺³⁺²ᵖ⁻¹ = (3²)⁽ᵖ⁺⁶⁾ 3³ᵖ⁺² = 3²ᵖ⁺¹² Comparing both sides- 3p + 2 = 2p + 12 p = 10 p² = 100

questions.question: In what ratio does the point (1,7) divide the line joining the points (-3,5) and (11,12)?
questions.explanation: Let the required ratio be m:n. (1,7) = ( 11m - 3n_________m+n , 12m + 5n_________m+n ) Taking x co-ordinate, m + n = 11m - 3n 10m = 4n m:n = 4:10 = 2:5

questions.question: The profit in a transaction is a% of the selling price and b% of the cost price. If 2b = 3a, what is the value of b?
questions.explanation: Let the selling price be Rs S and the cost price be Rs C. Hence profit obtained = S X a/100 .. (1) Also, profit obtained = C X b/100 .. (2) From both the equations, S X a/100 = C X b/100 => S/C=b/a => S/C=3/2 => S = 1.5C Thus profit = 50% Hence b=50%

questions.question: Production of cars in a company is increased by 25% in 2012 as compared to previous year and its sale increased by 20% in 2012 as compared to previous years. If Y% cars sold from the company in 2012 and then, number of cars remain unsold in company in 2012 is twice as compared to previous year, then find the value of Y.
questions.explanation: Let production in 2011 = m Let sale in 2011 = n Then, production in 2012 = 125% of m = 1.25m Sale in 2012 = (100 + 20)% of n = 1.2n And, 1.2n = Y% of 1.25m n/m = Y/96 Now, car remain unsold in 2011 = m - n And, car remain unsold in 2012 = 1.25m - 1.2n Then, 2(m - n) = (1.25m - 1.2n) n/m = 15/16 Therefore, Y/96 = 15/16 Y = 90

questions.question: In a family of 6 males and a few ladies, the average monthly consumption of grain per head is 14.4 kg. If the average monthly consumption per head be 20 kg in the case of male and 6 kg in the case of female, find the number of females in the family.
questions.explanation: Let k be the number of females in the family Total consumption of males = 6 x 20 = 120kg Total consumption of females = k x 6 = 6k kg Total average = (120+6k)/(6+k)=14.4 On solving this, we get k = 4

questions.question: If a = √3 + √2_________√3 - √2 and b = √3 - √2_________√3 + √2 , then find a²_________b + b²_________a

questions.question: A manufacturer reduced the cost of production of an item by 20%, but left the selling price unchanged, as a result of which his proﬁt (as a percentage of the cost of production) increased by 30%. What will be his profit percentage , if he reduces the cost of production by a further 25%?
questions.explanation: Let the initial cost price be Rs. 100 and the initial profit percentage is Rs. P. Hence, selling price = Rs. (100 + P). The decreased cost price will be 80. Final profit percentage = (P + 20) x 100/80 = P + 30 5P + 100 = 4P + 120 P = 20 Now, when the cost is decreased by a further 25%. Cost price = 80 x 75/100 = Rs.60 Final profit% = (120 - 60)/60 = 100%

questions.question: The lateral edge of a pyramid is 18 cm, and its height is 14 cm. The base of the pyramid is a square of side X cm. Find the approximate lateral surface area of pyramid (in sq. cm).
questions.explanation: Lateral edge = 18 cm Height = 14 cm Side of square = X cm Diagonal of square = X√2 cm Now, by Pythagoras theorem, (18)² = (14)² + [(X√2)/2]² 324 - 196 = X²/2 X² = 256 X = 16 Side of square = 16 cm Perimeter of base = 4 * 16 = 64 cm Side of square/2 = 8 cm Now, by Pythagoras theorem, slant height= ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/ssct2-22-598045sol.png) = √260 Lateral surface area = ½ * base perimeter * slant height = ½ * 64 * √260 ≈ 516 sq. cm

questions.question: If (a.secα - b.tanα) = 1 and (a².sec²α - b².tan²α) = 5, then what is the value of (4a² - 9b²)?
questions.explanation: (a.secα - b.tanα) = 1 ........ (1) (a².sec²α - b².tan²α) = 5 (a.secα - b.tanα)(a.secα + b.tanα) = 5 .... (2) From (1) and (2)- (a.secα + b.tanα) = 5 ....... (3) From (1) and (3)- secα = 3/a and tanα = 2/b sec²α - tan²α = 1 (3/a)² - (2/b)² = 1 = (9b² - 4a²) = a²b² = (4a² - 9b²) = - a²b²

questions.question: In the following question, a statement has been given with highlighted text. Select the option that can replace the text with the correct idiom or phrase. It was Allison's first day at her new school, and she felt very uncomfortable.
options: Like barking up the wrong tree
Like a fish out of water
To cost an arm and a leg
questions.explanation: IDIOMMEANING Adding insult to injuryDo something to make a bad situation worse Barking up the wrong treeTo be looking for answers in the wrong place Like a fish out of waterSomeone in an uncomfortable position or situation Cost an arm and a legTo describe something that is very expensive So, the correct idiom is 'like a fish out of water'. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given below. A person without manners or polish
questions.explanation: Rustic is an unsophisticated and mannerless country person Naive is an inexperienced person Ingenuous is characterized by an inability to mask your feelings; not devious Barbarian is a member of an uncivilized people Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Something capable of catching fire or exploding easily
questions.explanation: Inflammable means easily set on fire. Inevitable means unavoidable, soluble refers to something that can dissolve in liquid, and perishable describes items that decay or spoil quickly. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Has the proposal for restructuring the examination pattern been approved by the academic council?
options: Has the academic council approved the proposal for restructuring the examination pattern?
Did the academic council approved the proposal for restructuring the examination pattern?
Has the proposal for restructuring the examination pattern approved the academic council?
Is the academic council approving the proposal for restructuring the examination pattern?
questions.explanation: The subject and object interchange their positions while changing from Passive to Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb ["been"] and the preposition "by" is required. The Passive Voice sentence is in Present Perfect tense and interrogative form. The Active Voice must remain Present Perfect interrogative. Passive voice format: Has/Have + Object + been + V3 + by + Subject? Active voice format: Has/Have + Subject + V3 + Object? For example: Passive: Has the letter been sent by her? Active: Has she sent the letter? Only option (a) follows all the conditions correctly. Therefore, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank. He began to ______ the intricate web of clues.
questions.explanation: Unravel - This is the correct spelling. "Unravel" means to investigate and solve something complex, like a mystery or puzzle. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. The final sentence: He began to unravel the intricate web of clues.

questions.question: Some parts of the given sentence have been jumbled up, and labelled A, B, C and D. Select the option that gives the correct sequence in which these parts can be rearranged to form a meaningful and grammatically correct sentence. Doctors, unlike PhD scholars, specialize A. in medical practice, diagnosing illnesses, B. the advancement of knowledge in their field C. prescribing treatments, and performing surgeries, D. while PhD scholars focus on research, contributing to
questions.explanation: The sentence needs to start with a clause that introduces the subject. This is accomplished by part A, which talks about what doctors specialize in, setting the context. Following the subject introduction, we need to provide more information about doctors' specialization. Part C fits here as it elaborates on the tasks involved in medical practice. D should be placed next as it provides comparison by contrasting doctors with PhD scholars, explaining their focus areas. Finally, we need a closing statement or a continuation of the comparison made in part D. Part B serves this purpose by explaining what PhD scholars specialize in. Hence, (a) ACDB is the correct answer. The final sentence: Doctors, unlike PhD scholars, specialize in medical practice, diagnosing illnesses, prescribing treatments, and performing surgeries, while PhD scholars focus on research, contributing to the advancement of knowledge in their field.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Learning is the act of incorporating new facts, concepts, and abilities into our brains. B. And the more developed our understanding of the world is, the easier it is for us to adapt and pivot when our circumstances change. C. We start learning in the womb and we never stop; we are always developing new competencies. D. Every new bit of knowledge we acquire builds on what we already know and gives us a fuller, richer picture of the world.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a coherent paragraph is ACDB. Sentence A comes first as it introduces the concept of learning. It provides a definition or description of what learning entails, setting the context for the subsequent sentences. Sentence C then expands on the lifelong nature of learning, emphasising that it begins early in life and continues indefinitely. After establishing the lifelong nature of learning, sentence D explains how new knowledge builds upon existing knowledge, contributing to a deeper understanding of the world. Finally, sentence B concludes the paragraph by highlighting the practical implications of learning, suggesting that a well-developed understanding facilitates adaptation to changing circumstances. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Expedite
questions.explanation: The meaning of the word 'expedite' is 'make (an action or process) happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly'. Let's look at the meanings of the given options. Hasten: be quick to do something. Hinder: make it difficult for (someone) to do something or for (something) to happen. Pretend: behave so as to make it appear that something is the case when in fact it is not. Accelerate: to go faster; to make something go faster or happen more quickly. Among the given options, 'hinder' is the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Adroitness
questions.explanation: Dexterity - Skilful in performing tasks. Incompetence - Inability to do something successfully. Pompous - Self-importance. Diligent - Showing care and conscientiousness in one's work. The meaning of the word 'adroitness' is cleverness or skill. Among the given options, one that is closest to the meaning of the word 'adroitness' is option (a) 'dexterity'. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. I realized that she was a wolf in sheep's clothing when she backstabbed me during the selection process.
options: A harmful person pretending to be harmless
questions.explanation: "Wolf in sheep's clothing" means a harmful person pretending to be harmless. Thus, the answer is option b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The homophone for the word "knight" is _______.
questions.explanation: A homophone is a word that sounds the same as another word but has a different meaning and often a different spelling. Knight (a medieval soldier) and night (the time after sunset) are pronounced the same way but have different meanings, making them homophones. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Neat - tidy or well-organized. Knot - an interlacing of rope. Net - a mesh fabric or network.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank. She got some books from the library and immersed ______ in Jewish history and culture.
questions.explanation: Such a pronoun would fit in the given blank that can refer to the subject 'she' of the sentence and at the same time be the object of the sentence. Since 'she' denotes the female gender, 'herself' is the apt choice to convey that the girl bought book from library and got busy with them as 'herself' is a reflexive pronoun used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to a female person or animal previously mentioned as the subject of the clause . 'Themself' is used instead of 'himself' or 'herself' to refer to a person of unspecified sex. 'Himself' is used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to a male person or animal previously mentioned as the subject of the clause. 'Itself' is used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to a thing or animal previously mentioned as the subject of the clause. Hence, option (d) is correct. Note: Reflexive Pronouns: Reflexive pronouns include myself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, and themselves. These are used to refer back to the subject of the clause in which they are used: She hurt herself in the eye.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Garima has already finished her homework, __________?
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is 'hasn't she'. When the main sentence/clause is positive, the question tag should be negative, and vice versa. In the given case, the main sentence/clause is positive. So, the question tag would be negative. Also, if there is an auxiliary verb (be, have, do, is, etc.) in the main sentence/clause, the question tag also contains the same auxiliary verb. In the given sentence, the auxiliary verb "has" is used. Accordingly, option (a) is correct because it maintains the opposite polarity (positive main sentence, negative question tag) and uses the same auxiliary verb ("has"). Option (b) is incorrect as it changes the pronoun to "he" and uses "isn't," which is not consistent with the original sentence. Option (c) is incorrect because it uses "wasn't", which is not consistent with the original auxiliary verb "has." Option (d) is also incorrect. This option uses "doesn't", which is not consistent with the original auxiliary verb "has." The forms of 'do' (does, do, did) are used when there is no auxiliary verb in the main clause. When forming question tags, if the main clause lacks an auxiliary verb (be, have, can, will, etc.), a suitable form of "do" is introduced in the question tag. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. NOTE: Auxiliary verb: A verb that gives grammatical information that is not given by the main verb of a sentence. It supports the main verb. Auxiliary verbs help to form the various tenses, moods, and voices of other verbs. The principal ones are be, do, and have. Example: She will sing a song tomorrow. Here, 'will' is the auxiliary verb that supports the main verb 'sing'.

questions.question: Select the sentence which has no spelling error.
options: Working as a home-based entropreneur can be very rewarding.
She is a serial entreprineur and a business angel.
They were awaiting final distribution by the entrapreneur.
Success as an entrepreneur requires dedication, perseverance, and a solid idea.
questions.explanation: The spellings given in all the options, except for (d), are wrong. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The right spelling of the word is - ENTREPRENEUR and it means a person who organises and operates a business venture and assumes much of the associated risk.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The chief commander of the army will train the prince.
options: The prince will be trained by the chief commander of the army.
The prince is going to train the chief commander of the army.
The prince will be training by the chief commander of the army.
The prince would be trained by the chief commander of the army.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Future Indefinite. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in the Future Indefinite. The format of Active voice is: Subject + shall/will + V1 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + shall/will + be + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: He will complete the assignment. (Active Voice) The assignment will be completed by him. (Passive Voice) The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is (a): The prince will be trained by the chief commander of the army.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The passengers started to grumble when the flight was delayed for the third time.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym for "grumble" is 'complain'. 'Grumble' means to complain or protest about something. 'Complain' also means to express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something. 'Sanction' means to give official permission or approval for something. 'Revoke' means to officially cancel or withdraw something. 'Deny' means to refuse to admit or accept something as true, valid, or deserved. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What event marked the dawn of the Space Age, according to the passage?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the first paragraph, "Humanity's quest to explore space began in earnest during the Cold War, with the Soviet Union's launch of Sputnik in 1957, marking the dawn of the Space Age," it can be noted that the launch of Sputnik in 1957 marked the beginning of the Space Age. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: What has the International Space Station served as since 1998?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the first paragraph, "The International Space Station (ISS), a collaborative project involving multiple nations since 1998, has served as a platform for long-term research in biology, physics, and materials science," it can be noted that the International Space Station has served as a platform for long-term research. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: How have private companies contributed to space exploration?
options: Through reusable rockets and commercial tourism
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the second paragraph, "Private companies, including SpaceX and Blue Origin, are democratizing access to space through reusable rockets, reducing costs and enabling commercial tourism," it can be noted that private companies provided reusable rockets which reduced costs thereby enabling commercial tourism. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: What challenges are mentioned in deep space travel?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the third paragraph, "Challenges include the harsh radiation environment, psychological effects of isolation, and the enormous distances involved in deep space travel," it can be noted that the harsh radiation environment and isolation from humankind are the challenges involved in deep space travel. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: What benefits does space exploration provide on Earth?
options: Innovations like GPS and medical advancements
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the third paragraph, "Space exploration not only expands scientific knowledge but also inspires innovation on Earth, from GPS technology to medical advancements," it can be noted that innovation through GPS technology, medical advancements are some of the benefits of space exploration. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence. The director said, "How remarkable the team's performance has been throughout the entire project!"
options: The director said how remarkable the team's performance had been throughout the entire project.
The director exclaimed with admiration that the team's performance had been remarkable throughout the entire project.
The director exclaimed that how remarkable the team's performance has been throughout the entire project.
The director said that the team's performance was remarkable throughout the entire project.
questions.explanation: The given sentence in Direct Speech is an Exclamatory sentence expressing admiration. Such sentences are converted into assertive sentences in Indirect Speech using reporting verbs like "exclaimed with admiration". For example: He said, "What a beautiful painting this is!" (Direct Speech) He exclaimed with admiration that the painting was very beautiful. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct Speech to Indirect Speech: The reporting verb "said" changes to "exclaimed with admiration". The Present Perfect tense ("has been") changes to Past Perfect ("had been"). The exclamatory structure is converted into a statement. Inverted commas are removed. Therefore, the correct answer is: The director exclaimed with admiration that the team's performance had been remarkable throughout the entire project. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the word segment that can substitute the bracketed word segment correctly and complete the sentence meaningfully. Neither the manager (or his assistant were available) to answer the urgent client query.
questions.explanation: The error in the bracketed segment "or his assistant were available" involves two issues: first, the correlative conjunction "neither" should be paired with "nor," not "or." Second, subject-verb agreement with "neither...nor." When "neither...nor" connects two singular subjects ("manager," "assistant"), the verb should be singular. "Were" is a plural verb. a) "nor his assistant was available" - This option correctly uses "nor" with "neither." When two singular subjects are joined by "neither...nor," the verb is singular ("was"). This is the correct choice. b) "or his assistant was available" - This still uses the incorrect "or" instead of "nor." While "was" is singular, the conjunction pair is wrong. c) "nor his assistant where available" - "Where" is an adverb or conjunction of place and is not the correct verb form; it should be "was." The conjunction "nor" is correct. d) "or his assistants was available" - This uses the incorrect "or." It also has a plural subject "assistants" with a singular verb "was," which is a subject-verb agreement error. Note on subject-verb agreement with neither/nor: If both subjects are singular, the verb is singular. If both subjects are plural, the verb is plural. If one subject is singular and the other is plural, the verb usually agrees with the subject closer to it (proximity rule). For example: "Neither the manager nor the assistants were available." The correct sentence is: Neither the manager nor his assistant was available to answer the urgent client query. Hence, the most appropriate choice is option a). Note: Subject-Verb Agreement: Matching up singular or plural subjects with singular or plural forms of a verb is part of the process called agreement. This can be understood better by the examples given: - "She likes cooking." [singular subject and verb] -- "They like to cook vegetables." [plural subject and verb] Correlative Conjunctions are pairs of words that work together to connect two parts of a sentence that hold an equal value or correlate with one another. E.g., Either/Or, Neither/Nor, Not only/But also, etc.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Myself has / long since abandoned the notion / that higher education is essential / to either success or happiness.
questions.explanation: In the given sentence, 'myself' acts as a subject. We cannot use the reflexive pronoun 'myself' as the subject of a sentence. So, 'myself has' should be replaced with 'I have' to rectify the given sentence. 'I' is a subjective pronoun. Therefore, the error is in the part in option (b). The correct sentence is: I have long since abandoned the notion that higher education is essential to either success or happiness. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. Note: Reflexive Pronouns: Reflexive pronouns include myself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, and themselves. These are used to refer back to the subject of the clause in which they are used: She hurt herself in the eye. Subjective Pronouns: The pronouns I, you, we, he, she, it, and they are known as subjective pronouns because they act as the subjects of verbs: We went to the party.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error free, mark 'No error' as your answer. Plenty of developmental projects/ were undertaken/ by the current government of Canada
options: by the current government of Canada
questions.explanation: The sentence has no error. The noun in the case of 'Plenty' agrees with a singular or a plural verb in accordance to the sense they convey. In this context, 'projects' denote a plurality of the subject. So, the verb that must agree with it is also plural (were). Thus, option (d) is the correct answer. Additional Information: The nouns in the following cases agree with a singular or a plural verb in accordance to the sense they convey.Number Means or wages Variety Plenty A lot of or lots of One thirds of Most of Some of A half of Percent of More of Rest of
